content.full_text:
4515 FAIRFIELD DRIVE
CORONA DEL MAR, CA 92626
February 28, 1990

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O.BOX 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Gentlemen;

With regard to Proposition 73, of 1988, wherein it was

decided to include the Tax Collector's name on the Tax

bills. This refere to an elected office and the name of

the individual should be deleted from all tax bills, nor

should it be required on checks in payment. In our case

checks should be made out to "ORANGE COUNTY TAX COLLECTOR"

only. Since the average voter casts his ballot for the most

familiar name this becomes a lifetime job, and hardly a

fair practice.

Very truly yours,

John Fainberg

[illegible] DETECTIVE STAFF
NOTE TO [illegible] AND [illegible]
FROM [illegible] [illegible]


SUPERVISION BULLETIN INSTRUCTIONS
FOR [illegible]
[illegible] AT [illegible]


INVESTIGATION AND PREPARATION OF CASES FOR
[illegible]

It has come to my attention that too many cases are being prepared for trial
to utilize the services of a lawyer without adequate preparation. Cases should
not be tried and the client prejudiced by a lawyer with
no preliminary examination of his witnesses and
case law as prepared necessary to insure an ability
"INSTRUCT THE CRIMINAL ASSIGNMENT MAIL TO ALLOW FIFTEEN
DAYS AND SUFFICIENT TIME REPRESENTATION AND READ
OF FILED AND CONFORM TO OUTLINED PROCEDURE SHALL JUDICIAL
[illegible] FILED

[illegible] ANDY VIRUS VAGI
[illegible]
[illegible] ANDI

[illegible] ARMA
